Four apple trees, green and red of unknown cultivars. Found them too late in the year to sample their quality. May be on the grounds of a business. Access: Public.
3719 168th St NE Suite 2, Arlington, WA 98223, USA

3 trees, appearing ripe in early August. Some manner of green apples, with a yellowish apple in the middle. The flavor is extremely mild, but they have a good crunch and low mealiness. Behind a state farm office, but appear untended. Access: Public.

Beautiful blackberry bushes all around the roundabout. Safe spot to park nearby. There are two cultivars, a Himalayan (upright stalks) and a sprawling type near the little creek by the fence on the southern side. Not sprayed with pesticides as far as I know, a
Many small fruits. Described as lackluster. May need to bring weedwacker or machete to get through the bushes. Two other pear trees in the same stretch of road. Access: Public.
